content.thinking: The test is failing because the grapher widget switched from rendering Raphaël-based graphics to a Mafs-based interactive graph, so it's no longer finding the "Created with Raphaël" text. This likely happened when the grapher-to-interactive-graph feature flag was removed in a recent commit.

content.thinking: A recent commit removed the feature flag that controlled whether the grapher renders as an interactive Mafs-based graph or the older Raphaël-based version, so it now always uses Mafs. The test `should snapshot linear graph question` is failing because it's waiting for "Created with Raphaël" text that no longer appears, since the grapher is rendering a Mafs graph instead. I need to check what changed in the grapher.tsx file and understand the implications of removing that flag.

input.description: Show the grapher and feature-flag changes in HEAD

content.content:
commit b6e0e944eae04e9d4d445bf23591341670099441
Author: Ben Christel <benchristel@khanacademy.org>
Date:   Tue Jun 30 14:08:33 2026 -0700

    [benc/accessible-grapher] Remove grapher-to-interactive-graph feature flag

diff --git a/packages/perseus-core/src/feature-flags.ts b/packages/perseus-core/src/feature-flags.ts
index 99ff9ab631..771c42601a 100644
--- a/packages/perseus-core/src/feature-flags.ts
+++ b/packages/perseus-core/src/feature-flags.ts
@@ -12,7 +12,6 @@
 const PerseusFeatureFlags = [
     "input-number-to-numeric-input", // TODO(LEMS-4085): clean up feature flag
     "perseus-renderer-upgrade", // TODO(LEMS-4304): clean up feature flag
-    "grapher-to-interactive-graph", // TODO(LEMS-4259): clean up feature flag
     "desmos-calculator", // TODO(LEMS-4180): clean up feature flag
     "perseus-enable-point-label-field", // TODO(AITQ-385): clean up feature flag
 ] as const;
diff --git a/packages/perseus-editor/src/testing/feature-flags-util.ts b/packages/perseus-editor/src/testing/feature-flags-util.ts
index 146778c665..014e90643d 100644
--- a/packages/perseus-editor/src/testing/feature-flags-util.ts
+++ b/packages/perseus-editor/src/testing/feature-flags-util.ts
@@ -12,7 +12,6 @@ const DEFAULT_FEATURE_FLAGS: Record<
     // Real production flags.
     "input-number-to-numeric-input": false,
     "perseus-renderer-upgrade": false,
-    "grapher-to-interactive-graph": false,
     "desmos-calculator": false,
     "perseus-enable-point-label-field": false, // TODO(AITQ-385): clean up feature flag
     // ...add new flags here
diff --git a/packages/perseus/src/testing/feature-flags-util.ts b/packages/perseus/src/testing/feature-flags-util.ts
index 146778c665..014e90643d 100644
--- a/packages/perseus/src/testing/feature-flags-util.ts
+++ b/packages/perseus/src/testing/feature-flags-util.ts
@@ -12,7 +12,6 @@ const DEFAULT_FEATURE_FLAGS: Record<
     // Real production flags.
     "input-number-to-numeric-input": false,
     "perseus-renderer-upgrade": false,
-    "grapher-to-interactive-graph": false,
     "desmos-calculator": false,
     "perseus-enable-point-label-field": false, // TODO(AITQ-385): clean up feature flag
     // ...add new flags here
diff --git a/packages/perseus/src/widgets/grapher/grapher.tsx b/packages/perseus/src/widgets/grapher/grapher.tsx
index 978bb6fe55..e7fb73085f 100644
--- a/packages/perseus/src/widgets/grapher/grapher.tsx
+++ b/packages/perseus/src/widgets/grapher/grapher.tsx
@@ -626,10 +626,6 @@ class Grapher extends React.Component<Props> implements Widget {
     }
 
     render(): React.ReactNode {
-        if (!this.props.apiOptions.flags?.["grapher-to-interactive-graph"]) {
-            return this.renderLegacyGrapher();
-        }
-
         const interactiveGraphOptions = convertGrapherOptionsToInteractiveGraph(
             this.props,
         );

content.thinking: The feature flag removal means the grapher now always uses Mafs instead of the legacy Raphaël rendering, so the test that waits for "Created with Raphaël" text will fail since that text never appears anymore. I need to update the test to work with the new Mafs-based rendering instead. Looking at why only one test failed when both call the same helper — the second test with multiple graph types must still be rendering via Graphie, which suggests that `convertGrapherOptionsToInteractiveGraph` doesn't convert all graph types to interactive graphs, or the multiple types question falls back to the legacy renderer. I need to check the grapher render logic to understand which graph types get converted and which don't.

content.thinking:
So when there's a single available type like `["linear"]`, it converts directly to an interactive Mafs graph without any fallback. But with multiple available types, the conversion returns `null` since the interactive graph can't handle type selection, so it falls back to rendering the legacy Graphie-based grapher instead.

The failing test is now hitting the Mafs path, but it's still using `waitForInitialGraphieRender()` which looks for Raphaël text that doesn't exist anymore. The real issue is that the feature flag was removed in the most recent commit, so the grapher always attempts the interactive graph conversion now. I need to update the test to wait for the Mafs graph to render instead of the legacy Graphie renderer. Let me look for how other tests handle waiting for the interactive graph to appear in the DOM.

content.text: The `linearQuestion` has a single `availableTypes: ["linear"]`, so it now converts to an interactive (Mafs) graph, while `multipleAvailableTypesQuestion` falls back to legacy Graphie (which is why only the first test fails). Let me see how interactive-graph tests wait for rendering.

content.thinking:
The interactive graph renders synchronously with a figure role, so the grapher test for the linear question now needs to wait for that instead of the Graphie render. Since this is a snapshot test, a new snapshot will be generated when the deleted one is replaced, and the test will pass as long as it renders without throwing. The real problem is just the `waitForInitialGraphieRender()` call failing, so I need to figure out the right wait condition for the linear question.

The multiple types question still uses Graphie and passed with the existing wait, so that test is fine. For the linear question, I should update it to wait for the interactive graph to render. This isn't weakening the test — the most recent commit intentionally changed the production code to render an interactive graph instead of the legacy Graphie path, so updating the test to match that intended behavior is the right approach.

I'm thinking about whether to wait at all. The Mafs graph renders synchronously in React, and the TeX labels also render synchronously with the mock, so a snapshot should capture immediately. But to be safe and consistent with the pattern, I'll wait for the figure element to appear before taking the snapshot.

content.text: The interactive graph renders with `role="figure"`. Let me check if there's a shared helper for waiting on interactive-graph render, and confirm how the grapher renders the linear question (Mafs).

I see the issue now—the test needs to wait for the interactive Mafs graph to render instead of the old Graphie component. I should update the test to check for the interactive graph's figure element. I'm also considering whether the snapshot will be deterministic, since the interactive graph generates React IDs that should remain consistent in the test environment.

Only the first test needs to change since the second test handles a different question type that can't convert to the interactive format.

content.text:
This is clear now. The HEAD commit removed the feature flag so that a single-type grapher (`linearQuestion`, `availableTypes: ["linear"]`) now always renders as an interactive Mafs graph rather than legacy Graphie. The `multipleAvailableTypesQuestion` still falls back to legacy Graphie (multiple types can't convert), which is why only the first test fails. The commit forgot to update the test's render-wait helper, which waits for Graphie-specific "Created with Raphaël" text that no longer appears.

Let me update the failing test to wait for the interactive graph to render.

content.thinking: All the checks are passing—linting, type checking, and the full test suite with over 5,000 tests. The fix involved updating a snapshot test to expect the interactive graph renderer instead of the legacy one, since the feature flag was removed and single-type graphers now always use the interactive version. Now I'll draft the commit message.

content.text:
All checks pass: lint/format clean, typecheck clean, and all 318 test suites pass (5245 passed, 0 failed) with a freshly generated snapshot.

## Summary

The HEAD commit "Remove grapher-to-interactive-graph feature flag" made single-type graphers always render as an interactive Mafs graph instead of the legacy Raphaël/Graphie renderer. But it left `grapher.test.ts`'s "linear graph question" test calling `waitForInitialGraphieRender()`, which waits for the Graphie-specific "Created with Raphaël" text that no longer appears — so the test timed out.

I updated that test to wait for the interactive graph's ARIA `figure` instead. The "multiple graph types" test still uses `waitForInitialGraphieRender()`, since a grapher with multiple available types can't convert to an interactive graph and correctly falls back to legacy Graphie.

input.commitMessage: Wait for interactive graph render in grapher snapshot test
